meaning: adj.鏈夌己鍙ｇ殑,鏈夐敮鐘剁殑
examples: Let us watch and say jaggy things, in the hope that some of them will hurt.
Drive through pixellating smoke effects and jaggy shadows!
It is a very smart post-processing pass on the rendered image, basically selectively smoothing out parts of the image where a smart filter detects a jaggy line.
If it does have one edge it's a jaggy one -- we're talking about the rough renderings produced by that PenTile setup.
Measured over twelve months, the total sales made by a jaggy release schedule compared to a smooth schedule would likely be similar, but the smoother schedule has a key advantage.
